commit.message:
fix(benchmarks): run defaults_tp on CUDA and save progress

Forward device and eval_batch_size through the defaults_tp suite into
feed-forward NEF benchmarks so Colab runs actually use the requested
CUDA device instead of silently staying on CPU.

Move single-layer and multi-layer benchmark models and tensors onto the
resolved runtime device, synchronize CUDA around fit timing, and evaluate
accuracy in batches to keep GPU memory bounded.

Persist suite JSON and CSV outputs after every successful benchmark step
so long Colab sweeps keep partial results when interrupted.

Share device helpers from benchmarks.run with the recurrent harness and
fix hybrid incremental initialization so temporary NEF layers are created
on the active device.

Motivation: defaults_tp on Colab was slower than expected because the
suite ignored --device, and interrupted runs lost all completed results.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle user creation errors with password validation (#2)

* Add client-side password validation and improved API error handling

- Add inline password policy checklist (8+ chars, upper, lower, digit, special)
- Disable submit button until password meets all requirements
- Add ApiException class that parses error details from API responses
- Handle both 400 (validation) and 500 (server error) responses with user-friendly messages
- Guard HandleFormSubmit with server-side password check as fallback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: properly handle Keycloak error responses during user creation

- KeycloakAdminService.CreateUserAsync now returns KeycloakOperationResult
  with status code and parsed error message instead of just null on failure
- CreateUser endpoint maps Keycloak 409 to Conflict, 4xx to BadRequest with
  meaningful error details, and 5xx to 502 BadGateway
- Parses Keycloak JSON error responses (errorMessage, error_description, error)
  to surface human-readable messages to the frontend
- Prevents Aspire retry policy from retrying 400-level validation errors
  (which was causing the 3-attempt 500 pattern in logs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update history and add decision for Keycloak error handling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: pass Keycloak admin password to API and improve token error handling

The AppHost was passing the admin username but not the password to the API
project. Since Aspire generates a random admin password for Keycloak, the
KeycloakAdminService defaulted to 'admin' which was wrong, causing 401s.

Now reads the password from KeycloakResource.AdminPasswordParameter so the
API always gets the correct generated password.

Also improved EnsureAccessTokenAsync to log the actual error response body
before throwing, making auth failures diagnosable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Split frontend into ES modules

Separate monolithic index.html (625 lines) into focused modules:
- index.html: HTML structure + CSS (155 lines)
- js/protocol.js: wire protocol constants, message builders, parser
- js/state.js: shared mutable state (subscriptions, stats)
- js/ui.js: DOM rendering (book, info, log, subscriptions list)
- js/app.js: WebSocket connection, message handling, actions

Uses native ES modules (no bundler). Window-exposed functions for
HTML onclick handlers.

commit.message:
fix: race condition on track completion — old track re-streamed

Two bugs caused the wrong track to stream after auto-advance:

1. After _signal_track_completion sent update_playing_status(progress=end),
   the Ynison echo triggered false seek detection (drift ~2s), prematurely
   waking get_audio_stream before the actual index change arrived.
   Fix: update _last_progress_ms/time BEFORE sending so echo drift ≈ 0.

2. The premature wake read current_track_id from stale state (old track),
   streamed it at seek=end, then the real track change set seek=248s on
   the NEW track (from the echo of the second update_playing_status).
   Fix: extract _wait_for_track_change() that loops until current_track_id
   actually differs, ignoring same-track echoes.

game mode architecture. Discovered critical integration points for custom
game modes through Mono reflection analysis.

- MatchSetupRules (74 methods) - Rule/preset management, item spawning

5. Custom courses already supported by game architecture

Implementation Strategy:
- Hook MatchSetupMenu.CustomCourseData to inject custom modes
- Use Addressables for custom hole scenes (already proven working)

commit.message:
feat: add Day/Week/Month period toggle to chart view (#594)

* feat: add Day/Week/Month period toggle to chart view

- Add ChartPeriodData interface and periods field to ChartDataPayload in types.ts
- Rewrite calculateDailyStats() to collect up to 365 days using consistent pipeline
- Rewrite buildChartData() to compute daily (30 days), weekly (6 weeks), and monthly (12 months) buckets
- Add lastFullDailyStats field to CopilotTokenTracker for full-year data caching
- Add period toggle buttons (Day/Week/Month) to chart webview
- Update summary cards to be period-aware (labels, count, avg)
- Preserve selected period across background chart updates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: eliminate redundant getSessionFileDetails call in calculateDailyStats

Remove the duplicate statSessionFile + cache lookup that was happening for
every session file in the 365-day window. The SessionFileCache returned by
getSessionFileDataCached() already stores lastInteraction, so calling
getSessionFileDetails() on top was doing double the I/O work per file.

Also pre-warm full-year chart data in background after updateTokenStats()
so the chart panel opens instantly instead of computing 365 days on demand.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: open chart panel immediately; load week/month data in background

Previously the chart tab appeared empty for ~60s because calculateDailyStats()
(365-day scan) was awaited before createWebviewPanel() was even called.

Now the panel opens instantly with the 30-day data already in memory.
Week and Month toggle buttons show a loading indicator (⌛) and are
disabled while the full-year data is computed in the background. Once
ready, an updateChartData message enables them with correct data.

Also adds periodsReady flag to ChartDataPayload / InitialChartData and
CSS for disabled toggle buttons.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct period key mismatch causing chart to show black panel

The periods object in buildChartData() used keys 'daily/weekly/monthly'
but getActivePeriodData() looked up data.periods[currentPeriod] where
currentPeriod is 'day'/'week'/'month'. The lookup always returned
undefined, causing a TypeError crash in renderLayout() which was
silently swallowed by void bootstrap(), leaving the root div empty
and the webview panel black.

Fix: rename keys in ChartDataPayload (types.ts), buildChartData()
(extension.ts), and InitialChartData (webview/chart/main.ts) to use
the short form 'day'/'week'/'month' to match ChartPeriod.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(session): use retpath=www.yandex.ru for broad domain cookies

The library's refresh_passport_cookies uses retpath=passport.yandex.ru,
so the redirect chain never reaches yandex.ru — cookies only land on
.passport.yandex.ru. Quasar IoT at iot.quasar.yandex.ru needs cookies
on the broader .yandex.ru domain.

Bypass the library and do the auth flow directly with
retpath=https://www.yandex.ru, following the full redirect chain so
cookies are set on .yandex.ru (matching AlexxIT's original approach).

commit.message:
fix: use gh CLI and Bearer auth for setup action latest version fetch (#1921)

* fix: use gh CLI and Bearer auth for setup action latest version fetch

The 'Test Action (Latest Version)' CI job fails with HTTP 403 when
fetching the latest release via the GitHub API. The root cause is
that the action uses 'Authorization: token' header format, which
can be rejected on internal repositories.

Fix:
- Use gh CLI (pre-installed on runners) as primary method for
  resolving the latest release — it handles auth natively
- Fall back to curl with the modern 'Authorization: Bearer' format
  and proper Accept/API-Version headers
- Both approaches use the GITHUB_TOKEN already available in the env

commit.message:
refactor: decouple PTY terminal into src/pty-server.ts and src/public/terminal.js

Extract PTY WebSocket handler, ALLOWED_SHELLS, and getDefaultShell from
server.ts into a standalone pty-server.ts module exposing
setupPtyServer(server, verifyWsClient). Extract openPty/closePty from
app.js into terminal.js ES module loaded via <script type="module">.

- src/pty-server.ts: backend PTY spawn, shell allowlist, resize, cleanup
- src/public/terminal.js: xterm.js lifecycle, WS connection, keyboard shortcut
- server.ts: import setupPtyServer, remove ~65 lines of inline PTY code
- app.js: remove ~100 lines of inline PTY code, fix orphaned btnTerminal ref
- index.html: add terminal.js module script tag
- tests/unit/pty-server.test.ts: 6 tests for extracted exports
